mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust and expanding economy. This leads to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest PAYEMS value of 152544.0 indicates continued employment strength.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between PAYEMS 3-month change and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its cyclical low is a strong leading indicator of recession. This triggers a risk-off market response, causing negative equity returns as investors anticipate an economic contraction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
evidence_refs: fact:The current UNRATE of 3.5% is near a cyclical low, making this a relevant risk to monitor.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns, invalidating the signal's predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) lags, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This interaction creates expectations of future, more aggressive tightening, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 4% AND FEDFUNDS is lower than the YoY CPIAUCSL change.
failure_condition: A model incorporating the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not show a statistically significant improvement in predictive power over models using only the individual series.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms a durable economic rebound. This boosts investor confidence and corporate earnings outlooks, leading to strong equity market performance.
failure_condition: During periods identified as 'post-shock recovery', the correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing and supply-side activity. This serves as a leading indicator for broader economic slowdowns, prompting downward revisions to earnings and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate is negative.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 shows no statistically significant negative returns in the 20 trading days following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, which directly boosts revenues for consumer-facing companies and signals healthy aggregate demand, leading to positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following the release of monthly RSAFS data showing >0.5% MoM growth is not statistically different from the average return on all other days.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at a restrictive level, it increases the cost of capital for firms and makes lower-risk bonds more attractive relative to equities. This compresses valuation multiples and leads to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.58% is accommodative, so this mechanism is not currently active.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, the average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly lower than in periods where FEDFUNDS is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals falling demand in a key interest-rate sensitive sector. This acts as a leading indicator for broader economic weakness, negatively impacting investor sentiment and equity markets.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to exhibit negative returns on average in the 20 trading days following a trigger event where housing starts fall more than 15% year-over-year.
